matches.match_rationale: The core pain is consolidating heterogeneous campus data (different schemas, field naming conventions, enrollment DB formats) into a unified regulatory submission schema. This is precisely the ETL/schema-mapping use case — AI-assisted mapping handles the variation in how each campus labels the same concept, normalizes formats, and flags conflicts without manual re-entry. For a 500+ staff operator running 10-15 campuses, each campus likely uses slightly different spreadsheet templates or SIS exports, making schema mapping the highest-leverage intervention.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted ETL layer that ingests per-campus exports (CSV, Excel, SIS extracts), maps fields to a canonical regulatory schema, flags conflicts for human review, and outputs a consolidated submission-ready dataset — cutting the 40-60 hour cycle to a few hours of review.

matches.match_rationale: During consolidation, conflicting or implausible records across campuses (duplicate student IDs, enrollment counts that don't sum correctly, financial figures that deviate from prior cycles) are a primary source of filing errors and deadline delays. Anomaly detection can automatically flag records that deviate from expected patterns — inter-campus inconsistencies, out-of-range values, missing fields — before submission rather than after rejection. This directly addresses the 'reconciling conflicts' pain mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A validation layer that runs anomaly scoring across the consolidated dataset before filing, surfacing specific records and campuses where data looks suspicious, giving compliance staff a prioritized remediation queue instead of a manual audit of all records.

matches.match_rationale: The full consolidation workflow — pulling exports from 10-15 campus systems, applying transformation logic, reconciling conflicts, and populating submission forms — is a multi-step process that an AI task automation agent can execute end-to-end. The pain is not in any single step but in the orchestrated sequence: fetch → normalize → merge → validate → populate, with branching logic for conflict resolution. This is the canonical task automation use case and the only capability that addresses the full 40-60 hour workflow, not just a component of it.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agentic pipeline that triggers at the start of each filing cycle, connects to campus SIS/export endpoints, runs the transformation and anomaly-check steps, auto-populates the regulatory submission form with consolidated data, and routes only flagged conflicts to a human reviewer — compressing the cycle from weeks to hours.

matches.match_rationale: Some campus records arrive as semi-structured documents or reports (PDFs, Word files, formatted emails) rather than clean database exports. Entity extraction can pull student counts, enrollment dates, financial amounts, and campus identifiers from these documents into structured fields for consolidation. This addresses the subset of campuses that don't have clean SIS exports and currently require manual data transcription — a direct driver of both transcription errors and time cost.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An extraction layer that processes incoming campus reports in document form, pulling key regulatory data points (headcount by program, funding category, financial totals) into structured records that feed the consolidation pipeline — eliminating manual transcription for the non-database campuses.
